frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: dopamine encodes prediction errors driving associative learning

frameworks.name: sensory prediction error
frameworks.one_line: extends scalar value RPE to multidimensional identity-specific error

frameworks.name: population / ensemble coding
frameworks.one_line: identity information carried by pattern across dopamine neurons, not single cells

core_question: Do dopamine neuron ensembles carry information about the specific identity of a mis-predicted sensory event, beyond a scalar prediction error?
key_assumptions: dopamine transients support sensory-based associative learning
scalar RPE in single units cannot explain content-specific learning
downstream areas can read distributed firing patterns
manipulations: elicited sensory prediction errors via mis-predicted sensory events in rats
elicited sensory prediction errors in human midbrain via fMRI
key_findings: firing pattern across small groups of rat dopamine neurons signals the identity of the mis-predicted sensory event
human midbrain BOLD response to sensory prediction errors reflects the same identity-specific information
individual dopamine neurons and population averages do not reveal this content
interpretations: dopamine ensembles provide highly specific, multidimensional teaching signals
this opens new possibilities for how dopamine contributes to content-specific learning
